([/mɪdˈloʊðiən/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); [Scottish Gaelic](/wiki/Scottish_Gaelic_language "Scottish Gaelic language"): *Meadhan Lodainn*) is a [registration county](/wiki/Registration_county "Registration county"), [lieutenancy area](/wiki/Lieutenancy_areas_of_Scotland "Lieutenancy areas of Scotland") and one of 32 [council areas of Scotland](/wiki/Council_areas_of_Scotland "Council areas of Scotland") used for local government. Midlothian lies in the east-[central Lowlands](/wiki/Central_Lowlands "Central Lowlands"), bordering the [City of Edinburgh council area](/wiki/City_of_Edinburgh_council_area "City of Edinburgh council area"), [East Lothian](/wiki/East_Lothian "East Lothian") and the [Scottish Borders](/wiki/Scottish_Borders "Scottish Borders"). The modern council area was formed in 1975 when the [historic county of Midlothian](/wiki/Midlothian_(historic) "Midlothian (historic)"), also known as Edinburghshire, was altered substantially as part of local government reforms; its southern part formed a new Midlothian [District](/wiki/Districts_of_Scotland "Districts of Scotland") within the [Region](/wiki/Local_government_areas_of_Scotland_1973_to_1996 "Local government areas of Scotland 1973 to 1996") of [Lothian](/wiki/Lothian "Lothian"), whilst areas on the peripheries were assigned to other districts and the city of Edinburgh, which had always been autonomous to an extent, was formally separated as the [City of Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh_(district) "Edinburgh (district)") District. In 1996 Midlothian became a unitary authority area, using the same name and territory as in 1975.

**Midlothian** ([/mɪdˈloʊðiən/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); [Scottish Gaelic](/wiki/Scottish_Gaelic_language "Scottish Gaelic language"): *Meadhan Lodainn*) is a [registration county](/wiki/Registration_county "Registration county"), [lieutenancy area](/wiki/Lieutenancy_areas_of_Scotland "Lieutenancy areas of Scotland") and one of 32 [council areas of Scotland](/wiki/Council_areas_of_Scotland "Council areas of Scotland") used for local government. Midlothian lies in the east-[central Lowlands](/wiki/Central_Lowlands "Central Lowlands"), bordering the [City of Edinburgh council area](/wiki/City_of_Edinburgh_council_area "City of Edinburgh council area"), [East Lothian](/wiki/East_Lothian "East Lothian") and the [Scottish Borders](/wiki/Scottish_Borders "Scottish Borders"). The modern council area was formed in 1975 when the [historic county of Midlothian](/wiki/Midlothian_(historic) "Midlothian (historic)"), also known as Edinburghshire, was altered substantially as part of local government reforms; its southern part formed a new Midlothian [District](/wiki/Districts_of_Scotland "Districts of Scotland") within the [Region](/wiki/Local_government_areas_of_Scotland_1973_to_1996 "Local government areas of Scotland 1973 to 1996") of [Lothian](/wiki/Lothian "Lothian"), whilst areas on the peripheries were assigned to other districts and the city of Edinburgh, which had always been autonomous to an extent, was formally separated as the [City of Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh_(district) "Edinburgh (district)") District. In 1996 Midlothian became a unitary authority area, using the same name and territory as in 1975.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Midlothian&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

For the history of the pre-1975 county, see [Midlothian (historic)](/wiki/Midlothian_(historic) "Midlothian (historic)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5d/Modern_%26_Historic_Midlothian.svg/250px-Modern_%26_Historic_Midlothian.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Modern_%26_Historic_Midlothian.svg)

Map contrasting the area comprising Midlothian council (dark blue) within the historic county of Midlothian (light blue).

Midlothian County Council was abolished in 1975 under the [Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973](/wiki/Local_Government_(Scotland)_Act_1973 "Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973"), which abolished Scotland's counties and burghs as administrative areas and created a new two-tier system of upper-tier regions and lower-tier districts. Most of Midlothian's territory went to a new district called Midlothian within the [Lothian](/wiki/Lothian "Lothian") region. The Midlothian district was smaller than the area of the pre-1975 county, parts of which went to other districts:[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Currie](/wiki/Currie,_Edinburgh "Currie, Edinburgh"), [Balerno](/wiki/Balerno "Balerno"), [Ratho](/wiki/Ratho "Ratho") and [Newbridge](/wiki/Newbridge,_Edinburgh "Newbridge, Edinburgh") to the [city of Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh_(district) "Edinburgh (district)"); [Musselburgh](/wiki/Musselburgh "Musselburgh") burgh and parish of [Inveresk](/wiki/Inveresk "Inveresk") (which included the villages of [Inveresk](/wiki/Inveresk "Inveresk"), [Wallyford](/wiki/Wallyford "Wallyford") and [Whitecraig](/wiki/Whitecraig "Whitecraig")) to [East Lothian](/wiki/East_Lothian "East Lothian"); [East Calder](/wiki/East_Calder "East Calder"), [Mid Calder](/wiki/Mid_Calder "Mid Calder") and [West Calder](/wiki/West_Calder "West Calder")) and the Midlothian part of [Livingston](/wiki/Livingston,_West_Lothian "Livingston, West Lothian") to [West Lothian](/wiki/West_Lothian "West Lothian"); [Heriot](/wiki/Heriot,_Scottish_Borders "Heriot, Scottish Borders") and [Stow](/wiki/Stow,_Scottish_Borders "Stow, Scottish Borders") parishes to the [Ettrick and Lauderdale](/wiki/Ettrick_and_Lauderdale "Ettrick and Lauderdale") district of the [Borders](/wiki/Scottish_Borders "Scottish Borders") region.

The boundaries of the historic county of Midlothian, including the city of Edinburgh, are still used for some limited official purposes connected with land registration, being a [registration county](/wiki/Registration_county "Registration county").[[5]](#cite_note-5) The Midlothian [lieutenancy](/wiki/Lieutenancy_areas_of_Scotland "Lieutenancy areas of Scotland") area corresponds to the current council area rather than the historic county.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The last [lord-lieutenant](/wiki/Lord-lieutenant "Lord-lieutenant") of the county of Midlothian was made lord-lieutenant for the new district of Midlothian when the reforms came into effect in 1975.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The former county council's headquarters in Edinburgh became the offices of the new Lothian Regional Council, whilst the Midlothian District Council established its headquarters in Dalkeith.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 1991 the council built itself a new headquarters called Midlothian House at 40-46 Buccleuch Street in [Dalkeith](/wiki/Dalkeith "Dalkeith").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

The Lothian region was abolished in 1996. The four districts in the region, including Midlothian, became unitary [council areas](/wiki/Council_area "Council area").[[10]](#cite_note-10) The reconstituted [Midlothian Council](/wiki/Midlothian_Council "Midlothian Council") continues to be based at Midlothian House in Dalkeith.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Central government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Midlothian&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Central government")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ea/Midlothian_House%2C_Dalkeith.jpg/250px-Midlothian_House%2C_Dalkeith.jpg)](/wiki/File:Midlothian_House,_Dalkeith.jpg)

Midlothian House, Dalkeith

There is a [Midlothian constituency of the House of Commons](/wiki/Midlothian_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Midlothian (UK Parliament constituency)").

There was a [Midlothian constituency of the Scottish Parliament](/wiki/Midlothian_(Scottish_Parliament_constituency) "Midlothian (Scottish Parliament constituency)") up to the 2011 elections when it was divided between [Midlothian North and Musselburgh](/wiki/Midlothian_North_and_Musselburgh_(Scottish_Parliament_constituency) "Midlothian North and Musselburgh (Scottish Parliament constituency)") and [Midlothian South, Tweeddale and Lauderdale](/wiki/Midlothian_South,_Tweeddale_and_Lauderdale_(Scottish_Parliament_constituency) "Midlothian South, Tweeddale and Lauderdale (Scottish Parliament constituency)").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Midlothian&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/97/Glencorse_Reservoir_-_geograph.org.uk_-_412620.jpg/250px-Glencorse_Reservoir_-_geograph.org.uk_-_412620.jpg)](/wiki/File:Glencorse_Reservoir_-_geograph.org.uk_-_412620.jpg)

The [Glencorse Reservoir](/wiki/Glencorse_Reservoir "Glencorse Reservoir") in the [Pentland Hills](/wiki/Pentland_Hills "Pentland Hills")

The Midlothian [council area](/wiki/Council_area "Council area") contains the towns of [Dalkeith](/wiki/Dalkeith "Dalkeith"), [Bonnyrigg](/wiki/Bonnyrigg "Bonnyrigg") and [Penicuik](/wiki/Penicuik "Penicuik"), as well as a portion of the [Pentland Hills Regional Park](/wiki/Pentland_Hills_Regional_Park "Pentland Hills Regional Park"), [Rosslyn Chapel](/wiki/Rosslyn_Chapel "Rosslyn Chapel") and [Dalkeith Palace](/wiki/Dalkeith_Palace "Dalkeith Palace").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Demography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Midlothian&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demography")]

### Languages

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Midlothian&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Languages")]

The 2022 Scottish Census reported that out of 93,255 residents aged three and over, 31,635 (33.9%) considered themselves able to speak or read the [Scots language](/wiki/Scots_language "Scots language").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

The 2022 Scottish Census reported that out of 93,257 residents aged three and over, 712 (0.8%) considered themselves able to speak or read [Gaelic](/wiki/Scottish_Gaelic "Scottish Gaelic").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Midlothian&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Transport")]

Midlothian has a modern road network as well as some rural single-track roads. The [Borders Railway](/wiki/Borders_Railway "Borders Railway") runs between Tweedbank to Edinburgh, with four stations in Midlothian – [Shawfair](/wiki/Shawfair_railway_station "Shawfair railway station"), [Eskbank](/wiki/Eskbank_railway_station "Eskbank railway station"), [Newtongrange](/wiki/Newtongrange_railway_station "Newtongrange railway station") and [Gorebridge](/wiki/Gorebridge_railway_station "Gorebridge railway station").[[14]](#cite_note-visitscot1-14)

## Twin towns and sister cities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Midlothian&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Twin towns and sister cities")]

Midlothian is [twinned](/wiki/Twin_towns_and_sister_cities "Twin towns and sister cities") with [Komárom-Esztergom](/wiki/Kom%C3%A1rom-Esztergom "Komárom-Esztergom"), Hungary and [Kreis Heinsberg](/wiki/Kreis_Heinsberg "Kreis Heinsberg"), Germany. Since 1978 it is a sister city with [Midlothian, Illinois](/wiki/Midlothian,_Illinois "Midlothian, Illinois"), a suburb of [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago").[[15]](#cite_note-15)
